analysis.executive_summary: Documentation audit of docs.together.ai covered 231 pages with 100.43% crawl coverage and 91.5% overall confidence. Critical gaps identified: only 31.21% of 118 detected API pages have adequate usage documentation, 0.0% example reliability, and 10.82% freshness visibility. SEO/GEO issues affect 12.88% of pages. One confirmed broken internal link detected within user-facing documentation.
strengths: Comprehensive crawl coverage at 100.43% with 231 pages successfully examined
High overall audit confidence at 91.5% with strong link verification (95.0% confidence)
No repository navigation broken links; only 1 user-facing broken link detected
118 API pages successfully detected indicating substantial API documentation presence
Zero trap URLs or crawl scope issues encountered during audit
risks: Zero percent example reliability means code samples may be outdated, broken, or untested
68.79% of API documentation lacks adequate usage examples or integration guidance
Only 10.82% of pages display last-updated timestamps, obscuring content freshness for users
SEO/GEO issues on 12.88% of pages may impact discoverability and search rankings
1 confirmed broken internal link degrades user navigation experience
Lack of freshness indicators creates uncertainty about documentation currency for rapidly evolving AI APIs
Low example reliability combined with poor API coverage creates high friction for developer onboarding

prioritized_actions.action: Implement automated example testing and validation pipeline for all code samples across 231 pages
Create usage documentation and integration guides for the 81 API pages (68.79%) lacking adequate coverage
Add last-updated timestamps to all 206 pages (89.18%) currently missing freshness indicators
Fix the 1 confirmed broken internal link and implement automated link checking in CI/CD
Remediate SEO/GEO issues affecting 30 pages (12.88%) to improve search discoverability

limitations: External audit cannot verify accuracy of technical content, code correctness, or conceptual explanations
API coverage detection relies on pattern matching; actual API completeness requires internal schema comparison
Example reliability estimate cannot execute code or validate against live API endpoints
Freshness assessment based only on visible timestamps; cannot detect silent content updates without metadata
User experience quality, clarity, and developer satisfaction require human evaluation beyond automated metrics
